debrief.title: Malignant Hyperthermia Under Volatile Anesthesia
debrief.teachingFeedback: Classic MH: an uncoupled calcium release drives muscle hypermetabolism — EtCO2 rises first and will not clear with ventilation alone; only dantrolene turns off the process. All critical actions were completed without dangerous behavior. Time to treatment: 150s.
teachingPoints: A climbing EtCO2 refractory to hyperventilation is the earliest reliable MH sign.
Stop the trigger and run 100% O2 at high fresh gas flow while help arrives.
Dantrolene 2.5 mg/kg rapid IV, repeated to effect — cooling alone controls temperature but not the metabolism.
Each 10-minute delay in dantrolene measurably increases complications.
